_meta.notes: Each topic gets used once. The generator marks 'status':'published' and stamps 'publishedAt'. When only a handful of 'queued' entries remain, add more (or have Claude regenerate the pool). Hero images live in src/assets/. Video clips live in public/resources/.

topics.title: Ship a Text-to-Speech Prototype Before Lunch: The 10-Line ElevenLabs Quickstart
topics.angle: Practical walkthrough of getting from zero to a working TTS call using the ElevenLabs API. Cover auth, picking a voice ID, the POST /v1/text-to-speech endpoint, streaming vs file output, and one gotcha about rate limits on the free tier.

topics.title: The Voice Clone That Actually Sounds Like You: A Recording Checklist
topics.angle: Practical advice for recording the training sample: microphone choice, room treatment on a budget, script variety, how long the sample should be, and the three mistakes that produce robotic clones.

topics.title: Flash vs Multilingual vs v3: Which ElevenLabs Model Wins Your Use Case
topics.angle: Honest breakdown: Flash for sub-100ms agent latency, Multilingual v2 as the reliable workhorse, v3 for expressive long-form. When each is the right call and when switching costs more than it saves.

topics.title: Audio Tags: The Inline Stage Directions That Let v3 Actually Act
topics.angle: How audio tags work in Eleven v3 — whispers, shouts, laughter, pauses, emotional shifts — inline in your script. Worked examples showing the difference between plain and tagged input.

topics.title: Build a Customer Service Voice Agent in One Afternoon
topics.angle: End-to-end: pick a voice, wire up the knowledge base (RAG), connect to a phone number via SIP trunk, add turn-taking, test it with a real call. Highlight the Agents platform.

topics.title: Dub a YouTube Channel Into 12 Languages Without Losing Your Voice
topics.angle: How Dubbing Studio preserves the creator's voice identity across languages. Upload, auto-translate, voice-match, review, export. Cost and time per minute of output.

topics.title: Generate Any Sound Effect From a Sentence — The SFX Model You'll Use Daily
topics.angle: Text-to-SFX as a creative unlock. Examples: 'a rusty gate slamming in the wind,' 'a crowd gasping,' 'a dragon exhaling.' How it slots into game dev, podcast, and video workflows.

topics.title: Stop Paying for Royalty-Free Loops: Generate Your Own Podcast Music
topics.angle: Eleven Music for podcast intros, transitions, outro stingers. Prompting tips, genre tags, how long you can go, licensing clarity.

topics.title: Voice Isolator: The Click That Saves Every Field Recording
topics.angle: Removing wind, HVAC hum, café chatter from dialogue tracks. Comparison with traditional noise reduction. Real scenarios — docs, podcasts, shot-on-phone interviews.

topics.title: Conversational AI 2.0: Why Voice Agents Finally Stopped Sounding Like Voice Agents
topics.angle: The jump from scripted IVRs to v2 agents — turn-taking models, interruption handling, backchanneling, tool use, the expressive mode. Why enterprises are re-evaluating their support stack.

topics.title: Agent Workflows: Hand-Designing the Conversations That Actually Close
topics.angle: Visual workflow builder for multi-step agent flows. Branching logic, business rules, database hooks. A worked example: a loan prequalification flow.
